title: BLO Training for SIR 2026 Conducted Across Lunglei District
content:
115/2026-2027

Lunglei, 26 May 2026:Training programmes for Booth Level Officers (BLOs) under Lunglei District in connection with the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of Electoral Rolls, 2026 have been successfully conducted in phases for all Assembly Constituencies within the District.

The training sessions were organised from May 22 to May 26, 2026 at the DC Conference Hall, Lunglei and SDO (Civil) Conference Hall, Tlabung, covering all six Assembly Constituencies under the district — 30-Lunglei North, 31-Lunglei East, 32-Lunglei West, 33-Lunglei South, 34-Thorang and 35-West Tuipui.

The training programmes were conducted by Election officials and resource persons including Pi Lalhruaitluangi, Election Officer, Lunglei; Pu Beitlotha Nohro, DLAO, Lunglei; Pu Lalhruailiana Zote, BDO Lungsen; and Pu Moses Lalfakawma Tlau, SDO(C) Tlabung.

During the sessions, BLOs were imparted training on various aspects of the SIR process such as house-to-house verification, filling up of Enumeration Forms, identification of eligible electors, correction of electoral particulars and maintenance of accurate Electoral Rolls.

Enumeration Forms required for the revision exercise were distributed to all BLOs during the training. BLO Kits containing bags, water bottles and umbrellas were also provided to facilitate smooth field-level functioning during the revision process.

District Election Officer Pi Navneet Mann highlighted the crucial role of BLOs in ensuring that all eligible citizens are enrolled in the Electoral Roll and appealed to all concerned to extend full cooperation for the successful conduct of SIR 2026.

title: Lunglei District-ah SIR 2026 atana BLO-te training buatsaih fel a ni
content:
723/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 26:Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of Electoral Rolls, 2026 kalpui mekah May ni 22 leh May ni 26, 2026 inkar chhung khan Lunglei District chhunga Assembly Constituency hrang hranga Booth Level Officer (BLO)-te tan hlawhtling takin training buatsaih fel a ni.

Training hi Lunglei District chhunga Assembly Constituency paruk – 30-Lunglei North, 31-Lunglei East, 32-Lunglei West, 33-Lunglei South, 34-Thorang leh 35-West Tuipui te tan Lunglei DC Conference Hall-ah leh SDO (Civil) Conference Hall, Tlabungah te a hrang zelin hun ruk buatsaih a ni a. May ni 22 chawhma leh chawhnu, May ni 25 chawhma leh chawhnu leh May ni 26 chawhmaah te training hi buatsaih niin, Election Officer Pi Lalhruaitluangi, DLAO Pu Beitlotha Nohro, BDO Lungsen Pu Lalhruailiana Zote leh SDO (Civil) Tlabung Pu Moses Lalfakawma Tlau te'n training hi an pe a ni.

Training-ah hian BLO-te chu SIR kalpui tur chungchanga thil pawimawh hrang hrang – in tin tlawh chhuaka endikna kalpui dan tur te, inchhiarna (Enumeration Form) dah khah dan tur te, vote nei thei hriat theihna tur te, voter-te chanchin dik taka ziah dan tur te leh Electoral Roll dik leh felfai tak vawn dan tur te zirtir an ni a. SIR-a hman tur Enumeration Form bakah, BLO Kit (ip, tuithawl leh nihliap awmna) te sem nghal a ni bawk.

Lunglei District Election Officer Pi Navneet Mann chuan India khua leh tui kum 18 tling chinte kim taka electoral roll-a ziah luh an nih theih nan BLO-te chuan mawhphurhna lian tak an nei tih sawiin, SIR 2026 hlawhtling taka kalpui a nih theih nan theih tawp chhuah turin a chah.

title: CHAMPHAIAH MIZORAM STATE BIODIVERSITY BOARD LEH NATURE LEARNING CENTRE-TE BUATSAIHIN BIODIVERSITY CONSERVATION AWARENESS CAMPAIGN HMANG
content:
722/2026-2027

Champhai dated 26th May, 2026:Vawiin chawhma dar 11.00 khan Mizoram Biodiversity Board leh Nature Learning Centre-Mizoram te chuan biodiversity conservation awareness campaign chu CF Auditorium, EFCC Complex, Champhaiah hlawhtling takin an hmang.

He awareness campaign hi International Day of Biodiversity thupui ‘Acting locally for global impact’ tih hmanga neih a ni a. Champhai khawchhunga line department hotute, khaw hrang hrang Biodiversity Management Committee (BMC) member te, environmental NGO leh school naupang ten an hmang a ni.

He programme hi Pi Lalrammawii, Honorary Wildlife Warden, Champhai District chuan Khuallian niin a hman pui a. Khuallian chuan Mizoram chu Pathianin ramngaw leh nungcha chi tinrengin mal a sawm ram a ni a, heng kan nature mawina te hian khualzin tam tak ramchhung leh pawn atangin a hip mek a. Khualzinte hmuh chak ber kan ram mawinate,kan ramngaw leh nungcha te humhalh turin keimahni zawn theuhah theih tawp kan chhuah a pawimawh thu a tarlang a. A bik takin Champhai leh a chhehvel mawina chuan nature based tourism-lamah hmasawnna nasa tak a hmu mek a. Mipui ten kan chhehvel mawinate humhalh turin theih tawp a tan kan lak a apwimawh thu a tarlang a ni.

Pu Amit Kumar Bal,Research Scholar,MZU chuan presentation hmangin Murlen National Park leh a chhehvel khuate nena an thawh ho dante, nature conservation atana mipuite nena thawhho a pawimawhzia ngaihnawm takin a sawi bawk. He awareness programme ni Pu Lalbiakchama Chawngthu DFO Champhai, Member Secretary, Nature Learning Centre chuan a kaihhruai a ni.

Awareness campaign programme zawh hian Pi Lalrammawi,Honorary Wildlfie Warden chuan Champhai Forest Division staff te’n ramsa rep dan lo a zawrh an man khawmte hal ralna leh thingphun programme hlawhtling tak Champhai Nagar Van chhungah an hmang bawk a ni.

title: Prof. Lalnilawma Minister of RD&AD releases PAI 2.0 Report Card
content:
114/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 26th May, 2026:Prof. Lalnilawma, Minister of Rural Development & Administration Department (RD&AD) graced the "PAI 2.0 Report Card" release programme today as the Chief Guest. The event, organized by the Rural Development & Administration Department (RD&AD), Government of Mizoram, was held at the newly inaugurated Conference Hall of the Directorate of Local Administration.

Prof. Lalnilawma, Minister of RD&AD addressed  the gathering after inaugurating the new Conference Hall. He highlighted the utility of the Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) 2.0, an initiative of the Local Administration Department (LAD). The honourable Minister expressed his satisfaction with the release of the PAI 2.0 Report Card within this year, noting that the various PAI indicators will be highly crucial for community development. The Minister stated that as key leaders driving grassroots development, Village Councils (VCs) bear a heavy responsibility as the primary data collection sources through the PAI framework. He added that since the 9 themes of the Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs) are closely aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) implemented by the Central Government as framed by the United Nations (UN), it is imperative to remain mindful of their importance and be thoroughly well-versed in these 9 themes.

Prof. Lalnilawma expressed immense pride that no locality across Mizoram fell under 'Grade D' (Beginner) across the 9 themes. He noted that it is highly gratifying that under Theme 5 (Clean and Green Village), Kawrthah North VC secured a prestigious award from the Central Government. Deliberating on the 9 LSDGs themes, the Minister pointed out that there is still vast room for development across the country. He emphasized that these themes must be utilized as a guiding framework. He stated that to realize the Central Government's grand vision of Viksit Bharat 2047, progress must be driven from the grassroots level while keeping the LSDGs themes in mind, adding that operating with a close eye on the PAI indicators will usher in real development.

The Chief Guest remarked that VCs genuinely require empowerment, stressing that they still need extensive capacity building and quality training. He urged the departments supervising VCs to collaborate and make concerted efforts. Prof. Lalnilawma exhorted the assembled government officials and local leaders to consistently utilize the PAI 2.0 Report Card as the primary 'mirror' for self-evaluation to achieve further progress.

Dr. Lalngura Tlau, Director of LA, RD&AD, presented the Panchayat Advancement Index report and delivered a detailed explanation of the new PAI framework and its significance. Highlighting government initiatives, he stated that while the PAI 1.0 Report was released last year, PAI 2.0 is being released this year, and PAI 3.0 will be implemented subsequently based on the Financial Year (FY) 2025-26. PAI 2.0 uses FY 2023-24 as its baseline, whereas PAI 1.0 was based on FY 2022-23. He further explained that while PAI 1.0 comprised 516 indicators and 794 data points, PAI 2.0 has introduced a 71% reduction to streamline workloads, bringing it down to just 150 indicators and 230 data points, which has significantly eased data collection. As this collected data must be thoroughly validated by the Village Councils (VCs), the PAI initiative relies heavily on VCs, making it vital to collect data transparently.

Underscoring the importance of PAI, Dr. Lalngura Tlau stated that it is an essential tool to clearly monitor the year-round performance of VCs toward community development. He noted that the progress of various VCs can be viewed through the lens of the 9 Localisation of SDGs (LSDGs) themes. He added that it is a matter of great satisfaction that under the 5th theme, "Clean and Green Village," Kawrthah North VC from Mizoram secured the top position in India, receiving a cash award of 1 crore.

Based on their composite PAI scores, Village Councils are classified into five performance categories as follows:

The Director concluded his report by mentioning that the government will present recognition awards to the various Village Councils that score above 90 marks ('Achievers' or A+ Grade) across the 9 LSDGs themes.

The Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) is a comprehensive assessment tool launched by the Indian Ministry of Panchayati Raj to evaluate the performance and socio-economic development of Gram Panchayats across India. It serves as a continuous tracking mechanism for rural development and data-driven governance, helping to successfully realize the Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs).

Today's programme was chaired by Shri Lalchawimawia, Joint Director (Admin), LA, RD&AD, and Shri RC. Lalzarzova, JE, LA, RD&AD, led the invocation prayer. Short speeches were also delivered by Shri R. Lalremsanga, Director of SIRD&PR, and Shri FP Laikhama, President of the All Mizoram Village Council Association.

The event concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by Dr. Manesia Khaimeichho, Deputy Director, LA, RD&AD.

title: Health Minister Graces ZMC&H College Graduation Day;Inaugurates Interventional CT Scan
content:
113/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, May 26, 2026:Health and Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii graced the "Graduation Day" organized for this year’s graduating MBBS students at the Zoram Medical College and Hospital (ZMC&H). She also inaugurated an Interventional CT Scan, which is the first of its kind in Mizoram. Adviser to the Chief Minister (Finance) Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga and Higher and Technical Education Director Pu Laltanpuia were also present at the event.

Speaking as the Chief Guest at the Graduation Day ceremony held at Lecture Hall-4, Academic Block, ZMC&H, Pi Lalrinpuii congratulated the new graduates for successfully passing their exams despite having started their studies during the Covid-19 pandemic. She expressed her optimism that they would help bridge the gap in the state's shortage of doctors to a certain extent. She highlighted that the Government of Mizoram had made every effort to facilitate their MBBS education by employing quality teachers and covering their study fees. Therefore, she urged them not to run away when Mizoram needs their expertise and to fully honor the bond (agreement) that they had signed. The students had signed this bond before beginning their coursework, agreeing to work under the Government of Mizoram for two (2) years after completing their studies.

The Health Minister further encouraged the new graduates to prioritize subjects that Mizoram desparately needs—such as Anesthesiology, Biochemistry, and Microbiology—if they decide to pursue higher studies. She noted that continuous efforts are being made under the Mizoram Universal Healthcare Scheme (MUHCS) to ensure better and more accessible healthcare for the public, adding that it is a proud moment to have an Interventional CT Scan inaugurated in Mizoram for the first time.

Guest of Honour Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga expressed his appreciation for the hard work put in by previous governments to make a medical college in Mizoram a reality. He advised the new graduates to treat patients with empathy and to carry out their duties as doctors with a spirit of selfless service.

Dr. Lily Chhakchhuak, Project Director of the Mizoram Health Systems Strengthening Project (MHSSP), spoke about the newly inaugurated Interventional CT Scan. She explained that the u-CT 780 Model, 128-Slice CT Scanner System is an initiative under MHSSP, costing Rs. 8.18 crores, and is backed by funding from the World Bank. This CT Scan utilizes AI (Artificial Intelligence) and is designed to provide clearer images while emitting less radiation, making it particularly beneficial for cardiac patients, children, and cancer treatment. The machine has been installed in the Department of Radiodiagnosis. She also mentioned that under the MHSSP, CT Scan machines have already been placed in the Champhai and Siaha districts.

ZMC&H was established on August 7, 2018 and Post-Graduate (PG) studies commenced last year, with two seats available in each of five (5) departments: Anaesthesiology, General Medicine, General Surgery, Obstetrics & Gynecology, and Pediatrics, currently enrolling nine (9) students. Additionally, there are eight (8) students currently pursuing a Master of Public Health (MPH).

The new graduates belong to the third batch, having started their studies in February 2021. Out of the 98 students who appeared for the final exams, 96 successfully graduated, resulting in a pass percentage of 97.95%. Prizes were awarded to outstanding students, with Alex Vanlalruatkima, C. Lalhmachhuani, and Zosangpuii securing the first, second, and third positions, respectively. The Col VL Ruata Award was presented to Paul Ngurthantluanga Vanchhawng for excelling in various extracurricular activities beyond academics. Alex Vanlalruatkima also delivered a speech on behalf of the students.

The program was chaired by Prof. Zoramthara Zadeng. Prof. Swati M Patki, Dean of ZMC&H, administered the oath-taking for the new graduates, and Prof. Benjamin Lalrinpuia, Organising Secretary, concluded the event with a vote of thanks.

title: Health Minister-in ZMC&H College Graduation Day Hmanpui; Interventional CT Scan hawng bawk
content:
721/2026-2027Dated Aizawl, the 26th May, 2026 :Health and Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Zoram Medical College and Hospital (ZMC&H) a zirlai kumina MBBS zirchhuakte puala buatsaih “Graduation Day” a hmanpui a, Mizorama a hmasa ber ni turin Interventional CT Scan a hawng bawk. He hunah hian Adviser to CM (Finance) Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga leh Higher and Technical Education Director Pu Laltanpuia te pawh an tel a ni.

Lecture Hall-4, Academic Block, ZMC&H a Graduation Day inkhawmah khuallian Pi Lalrinpuii chuan graduate tharte chu Covid-19 hunlaia zirna tan mah ni se, tha taka an pass theih avangin a lawmpui thu a sawi a, doctor indaihlohna pawh engemaw chen chu a phuhruk ngei a ring tih a sawi. Mizoram sawrkar chuan MBBS an zir theih nan theihpatawp a chhuah pui a, zirtirtu tha tak takte a rawih bakah fee pawh a tumsak a, chuvangin Mizoramin an thiamnate a mamawh hunah tlanchhe mai lova, bond (inremna) an ziah chu puitlin vek turin a chah a, zirlaite hian an zir zawh hnuah kum hnih (2) chhung Mizoram sawrkar hnuaia thawk turin inremna hi an zir hmain an lo ziak tawh a ni.

Health Minister chuan graduate tharte chu an zir zawm dawn pawhin Mizoramin a mamawh tak tak subject - Anesthesiology, Biochemistry leh Microbiology etc., te thlang tura ngaihtuahna seng turin a fuih a. Mizoram Universal Healthcare Scheme (MUHCS) hnuaiah mipuite tan inenkawlna tha leh awlsam zawk a awm theih nan hma lak reng a nih thu a sawi a, Mizoramah a vawi khatna’n Interventional CT Scan hawn ani thei chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi bawk.

Khualzahawm Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga chuan Mizoram-in medical college a neih ve theih nan sawrkar hmasa ten nasa takin hna an lo thawh tawhna chu a lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a, graduate tharte chu damlote chu hriatthiamna nen enkawl a, tlawmngaihna nena daktawr hna chu thawk turin a fuih a ni.

Dr Lily Chhakchhuak, Mizoram Health Systems Strengthening Project (MHSSP) a Project Director chuan Interventional CT Scan hawn thar chungchang sawiin, u-CT 780 Model, 128-Slice CT Scanner System chu MHSSP hnuaia hmalak a ni a, cheng vaibelchhe 8.18 man niin, MHSSP World Bank tanpuina a kalpui a ni. He CT Scan hi AI (Artificial Intelligence) hmang niin thlalak fiah zawk leh radiation tlem zawk pe chhuak thei tura duan a ni a, lung lam thalo te, naupang te leh cancer enkawlnan a tha bik a ni. Interventional CT Scan hi Department of Radiodiagnosis hmunah bun a ni. MHSSP hnuaiah hian Champhai leh Siaha distrcit-ah CT Scan khawl dah a ni tawh bawk a ni.

August ni 7, 2018 a ZMC&H din tirh a nih atanga tun dinhmun a thleng thei chu a lawmthu a sawi a, nikum atangin Post-Graduate (PG) zirna tan a nih tawh thu sawiin, department panga (5)- Anaesthesiology, General Medicine, General Surgery, Obstetrics & Gynecology leh Pediatrics-ah seat pahnih theuh hawn ni tawhin zirlai pakua (9) an nei mek a, Master of Public Health (MPH) zir mek pariat (8) an awm bawk.

Graduate tharte hi batch thumna niin kum 2021 February atangin zirna bul an tan a, final exam pe zirlai 98 zinga 96 te chu graduate theiin pass percentage chu 97.95% a ni. Zirlai titha bikte hnenah lawmman sem a ni a, Alex Vanlalruatkima, C. Lalhmachhuani, leh Zosangpuii te chu pakhatna, pahnihna leh pathumna (an hming indawtin) an ni. Col VL Ruata Award pawh sem niin, zirna piah lama activities hrang hranga titha Paul Ngurthantluanga Vanchhawng hlan a ni. Zirlaite aiawhin Alex Vanlalruatkima chuan thu a sawi bawk.

He programme hi Prof. Zoramthara Zadeng chuan a kaihhruai a, Prof. Swati M Patki, ZMC&H Dean chuan graduat tharte chu oath taking neih puiin, Prof. Benjamin Lalrinpuia, Organising Secretary chuan lawmthu sawinain hun a khar a ni.

title: ECI SECRETARY HOVIN AIZAWL DISTRICT SIR (Special Intensive Revision) INPUAHCHAHNA THLIRHO
content:
720/2026-2027

Aizawl; 26th May,2026:Vawiin chawhnu khan DC Pisa Pui Hall-ah Election Commission of India (ECI) Secretary Pu Binod Kumar hovin Aizawl District DEO, EROs,AEROs,Supervisers leh BLO ten meeting an nei a Pu Binod Kumar chuan SIR kalpui tura in inpauhchahna te leh hma in lo lak tawh chin te hriat bakah, in hriatchian loh awm te, harsatna in nei te ngaihthlak a,ka ngaih pawimawh thurawn te pe tur che u a, vawiin meeting hi koh ka ni tiin, thurawn pawimawh tak tak SIR official kalkhawm te a hrilh hlawm a ni. Pu Binod Kumar chuan Electoral Roll felfai leh thianghlim neih nan a SIR (Special Intensive Revision) 2026 hi kalpui a ni a. Rampum nghawng pha thil a nih avangin a buaipuitu ten taima tak leh felfai taka in buaipui hi ECI chuan a beisei tlat a. ERO leh BLO te chu SIR kalpui tura dan leh dun awmte hre bel hle tur leh theitawp chhuah an duty hlen turin a chah hlawm a, enumeration form chu ni 5 chhunga semfel hman tum turin a chah a, duhsakna a hlan a ni.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian Aizawl District Election Officer Er.Lalhriatpuia’n SIR (Special Intensive Revision) kalpui tura Aizawl District inpuahchahna leh hma lo lak tawh chin PPT hmangin a entir a.

Aizawl District hnuaiah hian Assembly Constituency 12 awmin, Polling Station 324 a awm mek a. Rationalization hnuah chuan 359 awm tura beisei a ni. Voters 2,92,635 awmin, Mipa voters-1,36,654 leh hmeichhe voters-1,55,981 an awm mek a, Kum 2005 a voters awm zat chu 1,33,658 a ni thung. Electors mapped tawh hi 2,18,623 niin; chu chu 74.71% a ni. Enumeration form hi 5,85,270 print tawh a ni bawk.

Pu Binod Kumar hian DC Pisa Pui Hall-a meeting a neih zawh hnu hian Gov’t. Middle School, Zemabawk west a Polling station a tlawh nghal a ni.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian Pu Binod Kumar hi Pu Vijay Gupta, Section Officer, ECI in a tawiawm a ni.

title: KOLASIB DISTRICT CHHUNGA ASSEMBLY CONSTITUENCY THUM A BLO SUPERVISOR LEH BLO-TE TAN SIR 2026 TRAINING NEIH A NI
content:
719/2026-2027

Kolasib, 26th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Kolasib District chhunga Assembly Constituency thum (3) a Booth Level Officer (BLO) Supervisor, BLO leh Additional BLO te tan kumin 2026-a Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of Electoral Roll kalpui tur atana inbuatsaihna leh inzirtirna (training) pawimawh tak neih a ni.

He hunah hian Kolasib District Election Officer (DEO) leh Bawrhsap ni bawk, Pu H. Dolianbuaia chuan thu sawiin, Electoral Roll siam that leh endik hna hi thil pawimawh tak a nih avangin dik leh uluk taka thawk tur leh, langtlang tak  leh tangrual taka kalpui a ngaih thu a sawi a. Dik lo leh hming chuangbang awm lo, E-Roll thianghlim leh rintlak kan neih theih nan field level-a thawk tu BLO leh an supervisor-te chu a mawhphurtu pawimawh ber an nih thu tarlangin, theihpatawp chhuah turin a chah a ni.

He training-ah hian Kolasib district chhunga Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) zawng zawngte pawh an tel a, BLO te nen hian E-Roll siamthat hna an thawh dan tur leh mipuite finfiahna (verification) an kalpui dan tur te sawiho in hun tangkai tak an hmang a ni.

Training hi Master Trainer Pi Lalvenhimi Ralte leh Dr. Doris Lalrinawmi ten an pe a. Anni hian Electoral Roll endik leh siam that hna thawh dan tur kalhmang te, lehkha pawimawh (documents) thehluh ngai te leh finfiahna kalpui dan tur (verification process) chipchiar takin an zirtir a. Hna thawh awlsam leh tha zawk dan tur te tarlangin, hriat thiam loh zawhna awmte an chhang  nghal bawk a ni.

title: ECI Secretary Reviews Aizawl District’s Preparedness for Special Intensive Revision (SIR)
content:
112/2026-2027

Aizawl, 26 May 2026:This afternoon, a meeting was held at DC Pisa Pui Hall where Election Commission of India (ECI) Secretary, Shri Binod Kumar, met with the Aizawl District DEO, EROs, AEROs, Supervisors, and BLOs regarding preparations for the Special Intensive Revision (SIR).

During the meeting, Shri Binod Kumar stated that the purpose of the meeting was not only to assess the preparedness and progress made so far for the conduct of the SIR, but also to listen to any doubts, clarifications, or difficulties faced by the officials and to provide important guidance where necessary. He delivered detailed instructions and important guidelines regarding the official procedures for conducting the SIR.

Shri Binod Kumar stated that the SIR (Special Intensive Revision) 2026 is being carried out to ensure a clean and accurate Electoral Roll. As the exercise has nationwide significance, the ECI expects all officials involved to carry out their duties diligently, sincerely, and transparently. He urged the EROs and BLOs to thoroughly understand the rules and procedures governing the SIR and to discharge their duties with utmost dedication. He also instructed that the Enumeration Forms should be distributed within five days and conveyed his best wishes to all officials.

At the meeting, Aizawl District Election Officer, Er. Lalhriatpuia, presented through a PowerPoint presentation the preparations and progress made by Aizawl District for the conduct of the SIR (Special Intensive Revision).

Under Aizawl District, there are currently 12 Assembly Constituencies and 324 Polling Stations. Following rationalization, the number of Polling Stations is expected to increase to 359. The district presently has 2,92,635 voters, comprising 1,36,654 male voters and 1,55,981 female voters. In comparison, the number of voters in 2005 stood at 1,33,658. A total of 2,18,623 electors have already been mapped, which accounts for 74.71%. Additionally, 5,85,270 Enumeration Forms have already been printed.

After the meeting at DC Pisa Pui Hall, Shri Binod Kumar visited the Polling Station at Government Middle School, Zemabawk West.

Shri Binod Kumar was accompanied at the meeting by Shri Vijay Gupta, Section Officer, ECI.

title: RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma’n PAI 2.0 Report Card a tlangzarh
content:
718/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 26th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Rural Development & Administration Department (RD&AD), Government of Mizoram buatsaih "PAI 2.0 Report Card" tlangzarhna programme chu Directorate of Local Administration Conference Hall hawn tharah RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma chuan khuallian niin hun a hmanpui.

Khuallian RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Conference Hall hawnna a neih hnuin thu a sawi a. Local Administration Department (LAD) hmalakna thar Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) 2.0 chu ṭangkai a tihzia leh, kumin chhunga PAI 2.0 Report Card tlangzarh a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. PAI indicator hrang hrangte chu khawtlang hmasawnna atan an pawimawh hle dawn a ni, a ti. Minister chuan Village Council (VC) te chu khawtlang hmasawnna tura kaihruaitu pawimawh tak an nih angin, PAI kaltlanga data lakhawmtu mawhphurhna lian tak an nei a ni, a ti a. Localisation of Sustainable Development Goals (LSDGs) thupui (theme) 9 hrang hrangte chu United Nations (UN) duan sawrkar laipui pawhin a kalpui mek Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) nena kalkawp tlat a nih avangin, an pawimawhnate hriat reng a, he theme 9 te hi bel taka hriat chian tur a ni, a ti.

Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Mizoram chhung hmun hrang hrangah theme 9 hnuaia 'Grade D' (Beginner) awm miah loa kan awm thei chu chhuan tlak a nih zia a sawi a. Theme 5-na (Clean and Green Village) hnuaiah Kawrthah North VC-ten sawrkar laipui atanga lawmman hlu tak an dawng thei chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi bawk. Minister chuan LSDGs theme 9-te sawi zauin, ram pumah hmasawnna tur kan la ngah zia a sawi a. He thupuite hi min kaihruaitu atana hman a ṭul a ni, a ti. Sawrkar laipui hmalakna lian Viksit Bharat 2047 thlen thui nan LSDGs theme hre reng chunga grassroot level atanga hma kan sawn a ngai a ni tiin, PAI indicator-te en chungin hmasawnna a thlen theih tur thu a sawi.

Khuallian chuan VC-te hian empowerment (thuneihna leh chakna pek) an mamawh tak zet a ni tiin, capacity building leh training tha an mamawh nasat thu a sawi a. VC enkawltu department-te chu theihtawp chhuaha ṭang ho zel turin a chah a ni. Prof. Lalnilawma chuan PAI 2.0 Report Card hi hmasawnna tura kan inenletna 'darthlalang' pawimawh bera hmang zui turin kalkhawm sawrkar official leh tualchhung hruaitute chu a chah a ni.

LA, RD&AD Director Dr. Lalngura Tlau chuan Panchayat Advancement Index report ngaihthlak a ni a, PAI kalphung thar leh a pawimawhnate chipchiar takin a sawi fiah a. Sawrkar hmalakna tarlangin, nikuma PAI 1.0 Report chu tlangzarh a lo ni tawh thu a sawi a, kumin ah PAI 2.0 tlangzarh leh niin, nakum lamah chuan Financial Year (FY) 2025-26 base-in PAI 3.0 kalpui leh tur a ni, a ti. PAI 2.0 hian FY 2023-24 baseline-ah hmangin, PAI 1.0 chuan FY 2022-23 base-ah a hmang a. PAI 1.0-ah indicator 516 leh data point 794 zet mai a awm laiin, PAI 2.0-ah chuan hna tihawlsam nan za zela 71 (71% reduction) zeta tihhniam a ni a; indicator 150 leh data point 230 chauh neih a nih tak avangin data lakkhawm a awlsam phah hle a ni, a ti bawk. He data lakkhawm (data collection) hi Village Council (VC) ten an enfiah (validate) vek a ngaih avangin, PAI hmalakna hi VC-ah a innghat thui hle a, thu dik leh langtlang taka data lakkhawm a pawimawh zia a sawi a ni.

Dr. Lalngura Tlau chuan PAI pawimawhzia sawiin, VC-ten kumtluana khawtlang hmasawnna atana hna an thawh hriatchianna hmanraw pawimawh tak a ni, a ti a. Localisation of SDGs (LSDGs) thupui (theme) 9 kaltlangin VC hrang hrangte hmasawnna PAI hmang hian a hmuh theih a ni tih a sawi a. LSDGs hnuaia thupui 5-na "Clean and Green Village" hnuaia Mizoram chhung Kawrthah North VC te chu India ram pumpuia dinhmun sang tak an chelh thu leh nuai 100 lai an dawng chu lawmawm a tihzia a sawi a ni.

PAI score (composite scores) atang hian, khaw hrang hrang VC-te chu an hnathawh that dan a zirin hmun nga (five categories)-ah hetiang hian then a ni:

LSDGs hnuaia thupui 9 awm hrang hrang atanga mark 90 aia tam hmu pha, 'Achievers' (A+ Grade) khaw hrang hrang VC-te hnenah sawrkarin lawmpuina a hlan dawn a ni.

Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) hi India rama Gram Panchayat-te hmasawnna leh khawtlang dinhmun (socio-economic development) ennawnna hmanrua, Indian Ministry of Panchayati Raj-in a din a ni a. He hmanrua hi tualchhung hmasawnna (data-driven governance) chhui reng thin niin, tualchhung hmasawnna (Localization of Sustainable Development Goals - LSDGs) tihlawhtling turin nasa takin a pui a ni.

Vawiin hun hi LA, RD & AD Joint Director (Admin) Pu Lalchawimawia’n kaihruaiin, LA, RD& AD JE Upa RC. Lalzarzova chuan hunserh hun a hmang a. He hunah hian SIRD&PR Director R. Lalremsanga leh All Mizoram VC Association President Pu FP Laikhama ten thusawina hun an hmang bawk.

LA, RD&AD Deputy Director Dr. Manesia Khaimeichho chuan lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

title: CHIEF MINISTER ATTENDS VIGILANCE DEPARTMENT’S WORKSHOP FOR DISCIPLINARY AUTHORITIES
content:
111/2026-2027

Aizawl, 26 May 2026:A Workshop for Disciplinary Authorities organised by the Vigilance Department for disciplinary authorities from various departments was held today at the ATI Auditorium. The inaugural session was attended by Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma.

Addressing the gathering, the Chief Minister said that the Government aspires to have sincere, punctual and dedicated public servants, and accordingly, concrete measures were initiated soon after assuming office. He stated that a Service Review Committee was proposed in every department to identify non-performing officials, and where necessary, even special pension schemes may be considered. Necessary schemes for the same have already been formulated. He said these initiatives are aimed at ensuring more efficient governance and better service to the people.

The Chief Minister remarked that this is perhaps the first workshop of its kind in Mizoram for Disciplinary Authorities. He expressed hope that the workshop would strengthen authorities in taking lawful action against inefficient and negligent officials and help improve the quality of government service delivery. He urged Disciplinary Authorities to clearly understand their responsibilities, objectives and procedures, and to make productive use of the training.

He further stressed that Enquiry Officers must function strictly on the basis of facts, without bias or prejudice, and follow only fair and objective standards. He added that the Government would also exercise greater care in appointing Inquiry Officers. The Government has enhanced the honorarium for Inquiry Officers and Presenting Officers in line with Central Government rates, reflecting the value attached to their work.

Referring to the latest Vigilance Department Quarterly Report for March 2026, the Chief Minister noted that while some departments continue to have vigilance cases, departments such as Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Animal Husbandry & Veterinary, Art & Culture, Environment & Forests, Law & Judicial, Tourism and several others recorded NIL reports. He conveyed appreciation to those departments and urged departments with pending cases to work towards achieving zero vigilance cases.

The Chief Minister also thanked the Vigilance Department for organising the workshop.

The inaugural session was chaired by Dr. YLN Reddy, IAS, Secretary, Vigilance, who delivered the welcome address. Pu Sudhir Kumar, IAS, Acting Chief Secretary & Chief Vigilance Officer, delivered the keynote address. Following the inaugural programme, the participating Disciplinary Authorities attended training sessions on their roles, responsibilities and procedures.

title: Power & Electricity Minister Pu F.Rodingliana chuan Mizorama Solar Energy tih changtlun zelna turin Celebration of Solar month a hmanpui
content:
712/2026-2027

Aizawl; 26th May, 2026:Vawiin chawhma khan Power & Electricity (P&E) Department changtu Minister Pu F. Rodingliana hovin, Mizoram ah Nizung chakna hman tangkai zelna turin PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana (rooftop solar installation) inhriattirna leh Celebration of Solar Month chu Secretariat Conference Hall 1, MINECO-ah hman a ni.

P&E Minister Pu F. Rodingliana chuan thu sawiin kan ram ina mamawh ber chu Mahni Intodelhna tura hmalak hi a ni; tiin, chumi kawnga kan mamawh ber pakhat chu Power (kawphetha) a kan intodelh hi a ni a ti bawk. India ramah tun dinhmunah Mizoram chu PM Surya Ghar-ah chuan pakhatna a ni mek hi lawmawm a ti a ni.

Minister chuan kan ramin leilung nghenga tharchhuah hrang hrangte dahthat leh sawngbawlna lam a hmasawnna turte leh Industry lama hmasawn nan te Power hi kan mamawh a ni, a ti. Mizoramin hma a sawn zel theihna turin chak takin hma an la zel a, chu mi a bul ṭan nan Solar Installation Mizoramah tih mek a nih chu lawmawm a ti thu a sawi bawk a. Sawrkar building 70 chuangah Rooftop Solar dah tum a nih thu sawiin, tun dinhmunah Sawrkawr building 50 vel ah dah mek a nih thu a sawi bawk. Minister chuan "Kan ram ṭhan nan Power hi kan mamawh a, kan ramin a ṭangkai taka kan hman theih chu Rooftop Solar Energy hi a ni" a ti.

P&E Commissioner & Secretary, Pi K. Lalrinzuali chuan he hun hi kaihruaiin, Sawrkar laipui, Ministry of Renewable Energy in May thla chu Solar Month-ah a puan angin; vawinah pawh he hun hi hman a ni. Mizoram chu boruak a thiang a, Solar Energy siamchhuah a remchang hle a; sorkar laipui hmalakna ang in PM Surya Ghar scheme hnuaiah rooftop solar installation neih mek hi a hlawkpui tur chu consumer/mipuite bawk kan nih a avangin, mipuite hnena puangzar turin kalkhawm te a chah bawk.

P&E Department buatsaih Celebration of Solar Month-ah hian P&E Department Chief Engineer, (System Operation) Er. Laltlanthanga'n PPT hmangin PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojona chungchang tarlangin, Mizorama Solar Energy dinhmun report a pe bawk. P&E Department, Engineer-in- Chief Er. Ngursailova Sailo chuan thuchah pawimawh sawi bawkin, P&E Department, Joint Secretary Er. Laldawngliana'n lawmthu sawiin he hun hi a khar a ni.

He programme hi Mizoramah solar energy hman chakzualna tur leh rooftop solar installation chungchang mipuite hriattirna a ni a; programme zawh hnuah Secretariat Main Gate hnaih-ah vendor-te awmin, rooftop installation chungchang hrechiang duhte an lo pui bawk a ni.

PM SURYA GHAR: MUFT BIJLI YOJANA chungchang:

“He Scheme hnuaiah hian I inchungah Rooftop Solar dah ve la, Central leh Mizoram Sorkar atangin subsidy lo dawng ve ta che. Khawvel sik leh sa inthlak danglam zelah, kan chenna boruak tichhe lo zawngin Rooftop Solar hmang ngei hian I kawlphetha hman tur siamchhuak la, I hman bang te P&E Department hnenah rawn hralh ve ta che”.

title: Press Statement of Honourable Minister for Land Revenue & Settlement Pu B.Lalchhanzova on 26th May, 2026 at 12:00 PM at I&PR Conference Hall, Lianchhiari Run
content:
711/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 26th May 2026:Vawiin khan Land Revenue & Settlement (LR&S) Minister Pu B.Lalchhanzova chuan LR&S Department hmalakna chungchangah Press mite I&PR Conference Hall-ah a kawm a. Hetiang hian Press statement a siam:

1. Legislative Framework:(i) The Mizoram Land Revenue Act, 2013 chu August 2025 khan amendment pawimawh tak tak siam a ni a. Chung zingah SO tih thin AC-a thlakna te, ram awlsam zawka Sub-lease na te, Diversion of land use a DC te thuneihna pek te, dan lova ram luah chinfel dan mumal zawkte siam a ni.

(ii) The Mizoram Prevention of Land Encroachment Act, 2026 chu awmze nei leh chak zawka hman a nih theih nan March, 2026 khan Mizoram Assembly-in min pawmpui a ni.

(iii) The Mizoram Survey and Settlement Operations Act, 2026 pawh buatsaih niin Assembly fur thutkhawma pass hman tura beisei.

(iv) The Mizoram Land Revenue Act, 2013 kan hman lai chu hun inher danglam zel avangin tih danglam tulna tamtak alo awm tak avangin dan thar The Mizoram Land Revenue Act, 2026 chu buatsaih mek a ni a. Kumin chhungin peih fel hman a tum a ni.

(v) Apartment Ownership Act pawh duan mek a ni.

(vi) Revenue Court Manual pawh siam mek a ni.

2. Administrative Framework:(i) Thawktu lak belh (Staffing): Surveyor 14 lak belh niin heng mite hi District hrang hrang bakah a vawikhat nan Vairengte leh Thenzawl Sub Division ah te dah an ni a. Surveyor 30 dang lakna turin tunah hian hmalakna a kal mek ani. RFW 10 lakna tur pawh phalna a awm tawh ani. UDC-2, LDC-5, LDC (PE) 4 leh Group -D (PE) 2 lak belh an ni tawh bawk. Tunah hian LR&S hnuaiah hian Post awm zawng zawng 518 awmah a chanve 259 fill up niin a chanve 259 a ruak mek a ni.

(ii) Empanelled Survey Firm: Firm 13 te empanel an ni a, anni hian ram the hna an thawk a Department a surveyor indaihlohna phuhru tura hei hi kalpui a ni. Firm awm zat hian mipui mamawh a la phuhruk zawh loh avangin EOI siam leh a ni a. Hei hi tih fel theih tep a ni. Survey firm enge maw zat neih belh theih beisei a ni.

(iii) Licenced Surveyors atana ruahmanna: Mipui mamawh phuhru tur leh sawrkar tha ti chak turin Licensed surveyor neia chhawr turin hma lak mek a ni bawk.

(iv) Village Revenue Officer : Thingtlang khaw tin a theih loh pawn khaw tam zawkah Village Level Officer ruat ni se tiin ruahmanna siam mek a ni a. Revenue department hi mipui banphaka hnuh hnaih duh vangin hei hi ruahmanna siam a ni.

(v) Decentralisation: Mipui te tan awlsam zawkna a nih theih nan thuneihna sem zai a ni.

- Aizawl Revenue District chu North leh South-ah then a ni a. Director-in a enkawl thin veng leh khua te nen Aizawl district chu then a ni .

- DC-te Revenue thuneihna tam zawk pek an ni. Sub – Division-ah pawh thuneihna pek zel tum a ni a.

- A bultanna atan Sub-Divison 2, Vairengte leh Thenzawlahte enchhinna neih niin Office building leh quarters te sak a ni dawn a. Thawktu lam pawh dah tan a ni. Hemi bakah hian SRO anga hna an thawh theih nan thuam chak chhoh an ni ang.

- Land Committee, tun hmaa Sawrkarin a notify thin pawh DC ten an notify theih turin thuneihna pek an ni tawh.

- Diversion of land use ah pawh DC-te hi thuneihna thui tak pek an ni a, 5000 sqm chinah chuan thutawp siam thei tura thuneihna pek an ni.

- Sub-Lease of Land-ah Sorkar phalna lak thin chu kan Revenue Act Amendment siamin, DC-te thuneihna pumhlum pekin awlsam zawka kalpui theih tura siam a ni.

- Temporary pass renewal-ah pawh thuneihna pek an ni.

3. Mipui Nghawng thil hrang hrang – Citizen Services:(i) Area Survey/Resurvey: Mizoram state sawrkar hnuaia Revenue district 9-ahte khaw thlan bikah 'Bana Kaih' sum dawnin HA 266.120 zet survey zawh tawh a ni. Saitual town chhung survey niin plot 1879 zet survey a ni.

- Ramhlun Sports Complex, Mitla Mual, Bellei Mual, Kangmual, Sialsuk, Samlukhai te resurvey stage hrang hrangah an awm mek a ni. Maumual Phase II pawh bul tan tep a ni.

Chak zawk leh awmze nei zawka Area zau tak tak Survey zung zung theihna turin kan Survey and Settlement Operations Act pawh siam tha turin hmalak mek a ni a. Bill thar peih fel chhoh mek a ni bawk a ni.

(ii) Ramri khaw thenkhat Vairengte, Phaisen, Buarchep leh Saihapui V-ahte agriculture land record beihpui thlak tum a ni.

(iii) e-ram Portal: December 2024 a Chief Minister zahawm takin a tlangzarh a. Tihchantlun chhoh zel niin tunah hian Aizawl district-ah pilot a la kalpui niin district dangahte kalpui chhoh zel tur a ni. E-ramah hian application 10786 dawn a ni tawh ani. Mitinin rualkhai taka sawrkar an chhawr theih nan e-rama process zawng zawng chu first come first serve tura ruahman a ni a. Duhsak zawng leh thil dang avanga hmanhmawh bikte lo process hmasak theih a ni lo. Tarlan tawh angin e-ram hman a nih avang hian department chhungah kalphung engemaw chen thlak ngai a awm a. Tin, a software pawh han fel tawk lo lai a awm avangin Citizen lam pawhin harsatna engemaw han tawh chang a awm a, tin, zawhna nei te pawh a awm thin. Hemi atan hian tunah hian awlsam zawka mipui harsatna engemaw chen sutkian a nih theihna turin Whatsapp Bot leh IVR (Interactive Voice Response) buatsaih mek a ni a. Whatsapp Bot hi peihfel thawkhawt tawh niin hemi no : 84158 06438-ah hian 24x7 in harsatnate thlen theih a ni. IVR hi peihfel thuai tura buatsaih a ni bawk. Whatsapp Bot leh IVR hmanga kan harsatna sutkian a nih loh pawhin Whatsapp Bot leh IVR hian Office hun chhungin mihring lo dawngsawngtuah a redirect thei tho dawn bawk a ni. E-ram portal atang hian forthnightly-in land application status applicant tinte hnenah whatsapp message a thawn thin a ni bawk.

(iv) Stamps & Registration: hi State sum hnar pawimawh tak a ni a. Mizoramah erawh duhthusamin kan la hmu tha lo a. Heti chung hian nasa takin hma kan sawn a ni. State sawrkar chan liau liau anih avangin tan lak zel pawh a tul hle a ni. District tina Assistant Commissioner (Revenue)-te bakah SDO (Civil)-te chu Sub-Registration Officer-ah designate niin mipuiin Stamps & Registration an tih theihna pawh chhawp belh zel a ni. Kum li kalta chhunga Stamps & Registration atanga kan sum hmuh pun dan chu hetiang hi ani:

Kum Stamp Duty Registration Total2022/23 1,39,69,950 6,52,29,141 7,91,99,0912023/24 2,12,70917 6,26,58,938 8,39,29,8552024/25 7,22,32,505 6,32,366,346 13,54,68,8512025/26 12,34,10,859 7,86,48,303 20,20,59,162

Stamps & Registration hi State Sorkar sum hnar pui ber zinga mi a nih angin State tam takin tangkai takin ram lei leh hralh leh registration atangin sum an la lut hnem hle thin a. Mizoram hian hemi kawngah hian bul kan tan tlai deuh a, sum hnar pui takah kan la hmang lo deuh a. Sorkarin theih tawpa tan la chhovin, kum 2/3 ah chuan cheng 50 crore vel lak luh theih a, kum 5/6 ah chuan cheng 100 crore vel lakluh theih kan inbeisei a. Chumi atana hmalakna kawng hrang hrangte chu Sorkarin a kalpui chho mek bawk a ni. Hei hian Mizoram Sorkar sum dinhmun kal chho zel pawh tangkai tak a nih chhoh zel theih kan beisei a ni.

(v) e- Stamping: Mipui ten awlsam zawka stamp duty an pek a chumi atanga sum lut pawh felfai zawka lakkhawm a nih theih nan LR&S chuan The Mizoram Stamp(payment of duty by means of e-stamping) Rules, 2024 chu siam a nih hnuah Stock Holding Corporation of India-te nen May 11, 2026 khan inremna siam a ni a. He e-Stamping hmang hian mipui ten awlsam tak leh him takin stamp duty an pe thei tawh dawn a, he Stamp Certificate-ah hian finfiah nan Unique Identification Number (UIN) dah a nih avangin a lem leh phuahchawp lakah a him a, online hmangin a verify zung zung theih tawh dawn a, Record-te hi khawl (electronic) hmanga vawn a nih avangin bo, chhia, leh hmelhmang tihdanglam lakah a himin, chhiatrupna thlen pawha record vawn himna (disaster recovery) hmanga kalpui a ni. Lak chhuah leh enfiah a chak avangin lehkha pawimawh (documents) buaipui leh register pawh a chak phah. Tin, sorkar tan sum lakluh chhinchhiahna (accounting) a ti-awlsamin, Sawrkar hmuh tur sum dik tak a vawnghimin a chhinchhiah thin a ni. Physical Stamp paper chhutna leh buaipuina senso (printing & logistic) atan tha leh zung tam tak sen a ngai tawh dawnlo a, sorkarin a ruat Authorized Collection Centre (ACC), Common Service Centre – Village Level Entrepreneur (CSC-VLEs)-te hnen atangin hun tlemte chhunga lakchhuah theih a ni tawh dawn a ni.

(vi) Online tax payment: Ram chhiah hi kum tin khua leh vengah a thawktuten kalchilha khawn thin a ni a. Hei hi a hautak bakah tunlai khawvel hmasawnna mila ke pen a tulna avangin Online tax payment hi 2022 khan lo hman tan a ni tawh a. A software lamah siam belh leh tihdanglam ngai engemaw zat a awm avangin ICT dept nen thawk hovin Version 2.0 chu ni 29/5/26 hian tlangzarh mai tur peih fel a ni tawh.

(vii) Land Valuation siam that: Tuna kan land valuation hman mek hi kum 2015-ami kha a la ni a. Market-a ram hlutna leh LR&S-in ram hlutna chhutnaa hman chu a inkhawngrual lo nasa tawh hle mai a. Hei hian mipui tan kawng hrang hrangah harsatna a siam a ni. MZU Economics Department-te nen tangkawpin hei hi ennawn mek a ni.

4. Scheme/Programme pawimawh te:Revenue Department hian hmalakna pawimawh Scheme/ Programme thenkhat kan kalpuite lo tarlang ila -

(i) CM Land Reforms Package: Chief Minister in Land reforms pawimawhzia min hriatpuiin CM Land Reforms Package hnuaiah cheng Nuaih 500 min pe a. Hei hi Computerization of Land records thawh nan te, Resurvey conduct nan te, e-ram portal upgrade nan bakah SRO thuam chak nan te tangkai taka hman mek a ni. Hetianga kan Sorkar hotulu ber tena Revenue Administration tihchakna atana Package min ruahmansak hi kan lawm tak zet a ni.

He Scheme hi tangkai taka hmalakna atana hman mek a ni a. Hetianga thuam chhohna tur hi Revenue Department hian a la mamawh chho zel dawn a, chumi atan chuan kan mamawh ang te hi Sorkar ah thlen chhoh zel a ni dawn a ni.

(ii) Survey of Villages And Mapping with Improvised Technology in Villages (SVAMITVA) – He scheme hnuaiah hian Mizoram a Revenue survey la neihlohna khua 572 (319 + 253 ADC area) ah Survey of India nen tangkawpin Aerial Survey neih a ni a. Property card sing li (40,000) vel pek chhuah theih beisei a ni. He scheme kalpuiah hian Mizoram chu India hmarchhak state-ah a hmahruaitu niin India ram pumah pawh a che tha berte zing ami kan ni. SVAMITVA hmanga Property Card siamte hi LSC nen a in ang a ni.

(iii) Land Records Modernization - DILRMP: He programme hnuaiah hian Record of Rights digitization kalpui a ni a, tunah hian kan Land Record 3,61,188-te digitize tawh a ni a, map 2,01,785 geo-reference tawh a ni a, geo-referenced map 1,45,822-ahte ULPIN generate tawh a ni

(iv) Urban Land Survey & Records - NAKSHA: National Geospatial Knowledge-based Land Survey of Urban HAbitations (NAKSHA) hi Department of Land Resource bul tumin DILRMP hnuaiah he project hi 18th February, 2025 khan launched a ni a. He Project in a tum chu Urban Area ah drone/aerial survey hmanga land record leh property-te langtlang leh felfai tak leh tangkai zawka vawn a ni. Pilot atan India ram pumah 152 ULB-ahte bultan a ni. Mizoram ah pawh 18th February, 2025 khan India ram pumpui rualin Minister-in a Programme khuh hawnna a nei a. Mizoram pilot atan hian Aizawl Municipal Council Ward 11-19 (veng 43) thlan an ni a. Aerial survey hi GARUDA UAV ten neiin, heng veng 43-ahte hian Control Points te siamin Helicopter in LiDAR leh Photogrammetry survey neih a ni a. Drone hmangin aerial survey neih a ni a, hemi data hmang hian leilunga endikna Ground truthing kalpui leh mek a ni. Tunah hian veng paruk, heng - Luangmual, Dawrpui Vengthar, Dam Veng, Tuikual North, Tuikual South, Saikhamakawnahte Ground Truthing hi neih zawh tawh a ni a, Melthum, Kulikawn, Hlimen, Tlangnuam, Tlangnuam Vengthar-ahte neih mek a ni. Ram neitu bakah Local Council leh khawtlang hruaitute participation a pawimawh dawn em em a ni. Hetah hian LR&S department bakah UD&PA leh AMC-te nen thawh hona neih a ni a, data pawh kan intawm vek tur a ni ang.

5. Tlangkawmna:Mizoram Sorkar hian Revenue Administration changtlung leh tha neih chhoh zel tumin tan a la chho mek a. Revenue Administration hi thil harsa leh ken tel ngah tak a nih avangin awlsam taka siam fel zung zung a harsa hle a. Thil tam tak chinfel a, rem fel hmasak a ngai a ni. Chutiang tur chuan a dan lam, Legislative Framework-ahte, Sorkar chhung inrelbawlna Administrative Framework lam leh Mipuite hnena service kalpuina, Citizen Services lamte uluk taka ruahman chhoh a tul hle a. Tunah hian hmalakna engemawzat kan kalpui ve tawh a, hmalak chhunzawm tur pawh kan chhawp chho nual tawh a, chungte chu kan tarlang nual tawh bawk a ni.

Ruahmanna eng pawh mai hi a taka chang a, mipuite tana awlsam zawka service kalpui thei tur chuan thil tih ngai leh siam fel ngai a tam viau mai a. Sorkarina a ruahman ang leh duh ang hian thil a thleng zung zung thei lo lai hi a awm thin a, mipuite hriatthiamna pawh kan ngen a ni. Zawi zawiin a tha zawngin hma kan sawn chho zel ang a, mipuite tana awlsam zawk, chak zawk leh dawr nuam zawka service pek chhoh zel kan tum a ni. Tin, Revenue Department hi Sorkar tana sum thawk chhuaktu Department kan nih angin, State Sorkar tana State’s Own Tax leh Non-Tax Revenues-te a theih anga thawh chhuah zel pawh hi Department hmalakna pawimawh tak a ni zel dawn a ni.

Mipui tena heng kan hmalakna hrang hrangte hi min hriatpuia, min fin chhuah zel turin kan sawm che u a ni.

title: Hnahthial District Bawrhsap chuan Village Council Pali tan RGSA hnuaiah Computer Set sem
content:
709/2026-2027

Dated Hnahthial, the 26th May, 2026:

Vawiin khan Hnahthial DC Meeting Hall-ah Bawrhsap, Pi K. Vanlalruati chuan RGSA scheme atangin Village Council pali - Leite, New Kawnpui, Lungleng South leh Aithur te hnenah computer set pakhat theuh a sem a. District Local Administration Officer (DLAO), Pu HD Lalpekmawia chuan semna hun hi kaihruaiin computer set chu sawrkar laipui scheme - Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) atanga sem a nih angin VC tinte chu mimal tan ni lova khawtlang tana hmang ngei turin a chah a ni.

Bawrhsap chuan sawrkarin khawtlang hruaitute chu a duhsak avangin theih ang anga thuam that a tum thu a sawi a. Tun tum computer set dawng VC-te chu a lawmpui rualin computer chu thil chhe awlsam leh enkawl uluk ngai a nih avangin VC tinte chu a enkawltu tur bik ruat a, a khawih thiamte chauh khawihtir turin a chah a. Power supply tel lo chuan a tangkai tur anga a tangkai loh avangin backup nei tha tura chahin mimal tan ni lova khawtlang tana hmang theuh turin a chah nghal a ni.

DLAO leh DRDO ni bawk, Pu HD Lalpekmawia chuan vawiin a computer set an sem chu uluk taka enkawl a, tangkai taka hmang turin VC-te chu a chah a. Vawiin atangin District Panchayat Resource Centre (DPRC) ah DLAO anga hnathawh tan a nih tur thu sawiin khawtlang hruaitute'n inrelbawlna tha zawk an neih theih nan theihtawp an chhuah dawn a ni a ti a. DRDO hna chelh lai a nih angin LAD bakah RD hnuaia hmalakna leh hna hrang hrang awmte tha taka kalpui a nih zel theih nan khawtlang hruaitute chu thawhpuiah a sawm nghal a ni.

title: KHAWZAWLAH TEJAS AWARENESS PROGRAMME NEIH A NI
content:
707/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 26thMay, 2026 :Vawiin khan Khawzawl Addl DC Pu K. Zorammuana kaihhruaiin DC Conference Hall-ah TEJAS (Transforming Entrepreneurial Journeys Across States & Districts) Programme neih a ni a. He programme hi Startup India, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India, Mizoram Startup Mission (MSUM), Planning & Programme Implementation Department, Government of Mizoram leh Khawzawl Deputy Commissioner te ṭang ho huaihawt a ni.

Hun kaihruaitu Pu K. Zorammuana chuan Khawzawl chu district lian ni lo mahse mahni kutkawih thiamna (entrepreneurship) hmanga eizawnna leh district tihhmasawnna kawngah theihna sang tak a neih thu a sawi a. Tunah pawh sapthei atanga theitui siam te, nihawi atanga tel siam leh eco-tourism lamah te hmalak mek a nih thu a tarlang a. TEJAS chu Khawzawl mipui leh a bikin thalaite entrepreneurship lama tiharhtu atana programme pawimawh tak a nih thu sawiin, programme hlawkthlak tak hmang turin kalkhawmte duhsakna a hlan a ni.

Vawiin hunah hian MSUM atangin Pi Ruth Chuauthangpuii’n TEJAS programme chungchang sawifiahna hun a hmang a. Bana kaih Progress Partner Pu K. Lallawmsanga’n sapthei atanga theitui siam (juice processing) a tan dan te, hlawhtlinna a chan leh harsatna a tawhte sawina hun a hmang a. MSUM Research Analyst Pi Vanlalparmawii’n Start-up India chungchang inhrilhhriatna a nei a. MSUM atangin Pu Lalngaihawma’n Mizoram BanaKaih Venture Capital Fund leh Pu Lalremsanga hnen atangin GST chungchang leh sumdawngte tan sum lut leh chhuak felfai taka chhinchhhiah dan chungchang ngaihthlak a ni a.

Heng bakah hian department hrang hrang AH&Vety, Horticulture, Agriculture, Commerce & Industries, Geology & Mining atangtein Khawzawl district chhunga an hmalakna hrang hrangte thlirho a ni bawk a. Sawihona leh inzawhfiahna hun neih a nih hnuah SDC Pu R. Lalrinhlua chuan lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

TEJAS Programme thil tum ber chu sumdawnna lian leh hmasawnna hi khawpui lian chauh ni lo, district tina khaw tin thlen tir a ni a. District tinah ‘Start-up Ecosystem’ (sumdawnna thar thanlenna hmun) dina, khaw hrang hrang thil siam chhuah thiamna chu ram pum huapa hralh chhuah theih dan zawn a tum bawk a ni.

Vawiin TEJAS Programme-ah hian Khawzawl Higher Secondary School atangin zirlai naupang thahnem tak an tel a, khaw hrang hrang SHG-te, VCP leh Bana Kaih Progress Partner engemaw zat an tel bawk a ni.
